title: 2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL

content.text: 1. 1. Overview Download the Source code Build Source Code 1.1. Prerequisites Target Setup Flashing the SoC SW: Flashing SAIL SW Debugging the SAIL SW 1.2. A. Run the Debug cmd directly 1.3. B. Load the elf in t32 manually - deprecated
Overview
This page is to explain how to flash and debug SAIL in VCU PRO board
Note:  not need to install the Trace debug tool, it will be installed automatically when debug the elf file.
Download the Source code
We can download the source code using following command

content.title: Download source code
content.content: EMC_Pre_DV branch: repo init -u cm_gerrit:projects/taiji/manifests -b rb-ccufusion_hqx4560-es09_Pre-EMC -m rb-patac-ccufusion.xml -g sail repo sync -c -d --force-sync -j 8 2025.03.19 updated repo init -u cm_gerrit:projects/taiji/manifests -b rb-ccufusion_hqx4560-es09_Pre-EMC -m rb-patac-ccufusion_ssh.xml -g sail repo sync -c -d --force-sync -j 8 Main branch development - rb-taiji_main_dev branch 2025.05.07 updated We have moved the SAIL repos to Taiji platform so please use the below command to get latest main branch development code The below command will download entire Taiji code repo init -u cm_gerrit:projects/taiji/manifests -b rb-taiji_main_dev  -m rb-taiji-hqx121c1-pcs04.xml -g autosar,sail If you want Only SAIL repos use the below command repo init -u cm_gerrit:projects/taiji/manifests -b rb-taiji_main_dev  -m rb-taiji-hqx121c1-pcs04.xml -g sail Abandon: git clone --recurse-submodules ssh://git@sourcecode01.de.bosch.com:7999/pjw3/temp_j6e_mcu.git cd temp_j6e_mcu git lfs pull git checkout sail_bringup_dev git checkout sail_bringup (Obsolete)

content.text: Build Source Code
1.1. Prerequisites
Make sure to have license for Arm Fusa compiler version. Follow below mail to get license for Arm compiler
FW_ FSI Production Licen…

content.title: Build command
content.content: Enter this folder: temp_j6e_mcu\Cust_apl\Build and then execute the following batch file cmake_gen.bat -m -c

content.text: Once build is successful we have find the ELF file in below path
../temp_j6e_mcu\Build\DASY_win_R52_mcu
Target Setup
Board: VCU PRO A1 Sample
Debug board: VCU PRO debug board
Connect the Lauterbach with ARM connector as shown as below

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-14_10-56-38.png

content.text: Flashing the SoC SW:
Download the SoC Version from artifacts
https://rb-cmbinex-fe-p1.de.bosch.com/ui/repos/tree/General/patac-vcupro-repos/builds/rb-vcupro_main_dev/daily/rb-vcupro_main_dev_2024.36.1/rb-vcupro_main_dev_2024.36.1_userdebug.tgz
Follow the below wiki to flash the whole SoC binary
Install the Qualcomm package manager and download necessary software as mentioned in below wiki
SoC Flashing Guide With PCAT - GEN4 generic - Docupedia (bosch.com)
VCU Pro
Flashing SAIL SW
Step 1:
A.Use the folder in the repo directly - recommend
After executing building，all SAIL flashing related files will be automaticly updated in the path: \cust_apl\build\Qcimages\Prog_Sail
Simply use this folder in Step 2 to flash the SAIL

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2025-8-15_10-49-44.png

content.text: B.Copy elf file manually - deprecated
Copy the signed elf file from build repo and paste to build images with name as sailsw1.elf
make sure you copy the signed elf. If you copy the normal elf, hypervisor will not load the El1 elf into SRAM.

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-10-16_20-30-17.png

content.text: Paste in below folder where we downloaded SOC binary

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-10-16_20-31-10.png

content.text: Copy the Hypervisor elf from repo and paste to Sail image folder

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-10-10_16-56-27.png

content.text: If the hypervisor image is not flashed with the one in repo, the code will not jump to EL1 SW
Step 2: Configure the PCAT and flash the SAIL Images
Do steps 1 to 5 as shown in below image

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-14_11-2-55.png

content.text: we should see Build loading completed on the device
Debugging the SAIL SW
1.2. A. Run the Debug cmd directly
1.3. B. Load the elf in t32 manually - deprecated
Step 1: Open t32start.exe from any of the trace32 installation folder - from antifactory or from J6E repo
Select Power view instance as Arm and click on start

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-14_11-7-15.png

content.text: Now the T32 Powerview window will be open as shown below

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-14_11-8-16.png

content.text: Step 2: Configure DAP settings
In T32 Power window, run the below attached CMM script → this will configure required DAP settings
SAIL_Connect_XC.cmm

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-14_11-9-56.png

content.text: Click Run script and choose the downloaded file
No Errors should be there and there will be a Cortex R52 option in menu bar

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-14_11-12-23.png

content.text: Step 3: Connect to CPU
click on CPU → In the System window click on Attach , now the Mode will be automatically switch to Up and you can see running state in bottom right corner as marked in below picture

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-14_11-13-45.png

content.text: Step 4: Load the elf

items: Break the execution by clicking the Pause button in menu bar
Now load the elf using the below command

content.text: Data.load.elf elf_file_path /Nocode
elf_file_name = ..\temp_j6e_mcu\Build\DASY_win_R52_mcu\DASY_win_R52_mcu.elf
Example: Data.load.elf C:\GIT\Sail_J6E_Base\temp_j6e_mcu\Build\DASY_win_R52_mcu\DASY_win_R52_mcu.elf /Nocode
Now we can see the execution will be the while loop. Change PC to main function and then we can see OS tasks will be scheduled.

content.local_path: _images/2.0 SAIL - How to Flash and debug SAIL/image-2024-9-29_13-45-47.png

content.text: To reset and debug again → just follow step 4 after power reset
